affordances.name: Prune work to leverage drivers
affordances.mechanism: When many tasks look valuable but only a few move outcome quality, use Tier-2 High Value to distinguish leverage from busyness and define the end product that resolves the issue.

use_when: Many tasks look valuable on the surface but only a few meaningfully move the outcome, decision quality, or learning rate.
The team can distinguish leverage from busyness and define the specific end-product that would resolve the issue.
Time, attention, or capital are scarce enough that ruthless pruning matters more than completeness theater.
do_not_use_when: People talk fluently about leverage, strategy, or value while skipping concrete enabling details.
High-level framing creates a false sense that the hard thinking is complete.
Tier-2 language is used as a quality rank without case-specific fit.
case_evidence_needed: The outcome, decision quality, or learning rate that matters.
The small set of leverage drivers or high-impact activities.
The end product that would resolve the issue.
The enabling details, ownership, sequencing, or evidence that make the leverage real.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to name the leverage driver, the resolving end product, and the enabling details so high-value framing becomes executable.
evidence_required: The end product or decision that resolves the issue.
The concrete enabling details, ownership, and sequence.
good_output_shape: Adds the concrete enabling details needed for execution.

diagnostic_questions: Which few actions actually move the outcome, decision quality, or learning rate?
What end product would resolve the issue rather than just keep work moving?
Are we using high-value language while skipping the concrete enabling details?
misuse_guards: Do not treat Tier 2 as a status label or quality rank.
Do not let leverage language skip ownership, sequence, and evidence.
Do not confuse high-level framing with finished thinking.

source_evidence.source_quote: many tasks look valuable on the surface but only a few meaningfully move the outcome
distinguish leverage from busyness and define the specific end-product
skipping the concrete enabling details that make the recommendation executable

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 17 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports high-value pruning only when leverage drivers are connected to executable end products.
dropped_material: Did not treat Tier 2 as a universal importance rank.
Did not convert the model into deterministic prioritization.
open_questions: Later packet review should inspect overlap with opportunity-cost and leverage-points.

absence_records.reason: The source defines Tier-2 value through case-specific leverage drivers; it does not support treating the label as a quality rank.

source_evidence.source_quote: distinguish between the truly crucial elements and the peripheral noise

absence_records.reason: The source requires the specific end product and leverage logic for the case; it does not support high-value claims without fit.

source_evidence.source_quote: define the specific end-product that would actually resolve the issue
focusing energy and resources exclusively on the core drivers

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 17 keeps Tier-2 High Value as leverage pruning tied to a case-specific end product.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, prioritization scorer, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: A future packet should test whether this card helps prune candidate advice without over-centralizing the packet around leverage language.
